macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US Retail Sales Surge in March, Exceeding Expectations
items.summary: US headline retail sales rose 1.7% month-over-month in March, the strongest increase in over three years, while core retail sales climbed 1.9%, signaling resilient consumer spending amid rising energy inflation pressures.[1]

items.title: UK Labor Market Shows Mixed Signals with Lower Unemployment
items.summary: UK unemployment rate unexpectedly fell to 4.9% in February, but job growth slowed, early March payrolls dropped 11K, vacancies hit 711K, and regular pay growth eased to 3.6%.[1]

items.title: GBP/USD Bullish Amid Economic Data, Eyes 1.37 Breakout
items.summary: GBP/USD forms bullish flag pattern targeting 1.37+ if 1.3600 resistance breaks, driven by strong US retail sales and UK labor data, despite Middle East tensions.[1]

items.title: Fed Waller Speech Scheduled for 6:30 PM UTC
items.summary: Upcoming US Fed Governor Waller speech at 6:30 PM UTC, amid focus on economic data overshadowing US-Iran conflict developments.[2]

items.title: US API Crude Oil Stock Change at 6:30 PM UTC
items.summary: US API Crude Oil Stock Change report due at 6:30 PM UTC, following recent natural gas and refinery data releases.[2]

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Above $78K on US-Iran Ceasefire Extension
items.summary: Bitcoin climbed above $78,000 to an 11-week high, up around 2% in the past 24 hours and over 5% weekly, driven by President Trump's indefinite ceasefire extension with Iran. Ethereum rose roughly 3% over the same period amid positive market risk appetite.

items.title: Bitcoin Rallies 10% in 24 Hours on Extended US-Iran Ceasefire
items.summary: Bitcoin rallied over 10% in the past 24 hours, pushing the total crypto market cap above $2.7 trillion as the extended US-Iran ceasefire boosted market risk appetite.

items.title: Meme Coins Lead Crypto Sector with 4.81% Gain Amid Mixed Performance
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market showed mixed results over the past 24 hours, with the Meme sector notably rising 4.81%.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Sony AI Unveils Autonomous Ping Pong Robot Achieving Expert-Level Performance
items.summary: Sony AI announced its autonomous ping pong robot as the first to attain expert-level performance in a physical sport, defeating top human players. This breakthrough demonstrates advanced AI capabilities in real-world physical tasks.

items.title: Rise of Open-Weight AI Models Highlighted in Daily AI News
items.summary: GAI Insights reports on the growing prominence of open-weight models and AI sovereignties as a key trend in generative AI. This development is seen as increasingly important in the evolving AI landscape.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Crimson Desert Map Expansion Appears Likely After Out-of-Bounds Discoveries
items.summary: Pearl Abyss has developed extensive lands beyond the current map borders in Crimson Desert, with out-of-bounds videos revealing ruins, outposts, and enemies, suggesting future map expansions are a reality.

items.title: Destiny 1 Streams Surge with Major News Developments
items.summary: A popular Destiny 1 stream garnered 86K views in the past 16 hours amid massive news, including discussions on Bungie's looter shooter alongside broader gaming industry updates like 'Stop Killing Games' ruling in Europe.

items.title: Anime Vanguards Faces Significant Balancing Issues in Latest Update
items.summary: Roblox game Anime Vanguards is criticized for poor unit balancing, where some units dominate from early to end game via macroing, hindering actual gameplay and player progression.

items.title: Scourge Sky Grave Match Highlights StarCraft Esports Action
items.summary: Recent StarCraft II esports cast features Light (T) vs Shine (Z) on Sylphid 3 map, drawing views just 3 hours ago in ongoing competitive scene.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Anker Unveils Thus Compute-in-Memory Chip Platform for On-Device Processing
items.summary: Anker introduced its THUS AI chip platform, designed to bring neural-network features to consumer devices like upcoming Soundcore earbuds by embedding processing closer to memory for lower energy use. This reflects a trend toward edge computing in accessories and appliances beyond phones and laptops.[1][3]

items.title: Google Launches Eighth-Generation TPUs Including TPU 8t and 8i
items.summary: Google Cloud unveiled its latest tensor processing units, TPU 8t for training and TPU 8i for inference, set for general availability later this year. Investors view this as increasing competitive pressure on Nvidia's dominance in the chip market.[1][3]

items.title: HCLTech Reports Q4 Net Profit Up 4.2% to Rs 4,488 Crore
items.summary: HCLTech announced a 4.2% year-over-year increase in Q4 net profit to Rs 4,488 crore, with revenue rising 12%, and declared a Rs 24 per share dividend. This comes amid discussions on CEO transitions at peers like Infosys.[2]

items.title: Researchers Demonstrate Manufacturing Tweak for More Stable Perovskite Solar Cells
items.summary: Scientists from Korea University and the University of Surrey showed a simple technique that improves performance and stability of next-generation perovskite solar cells without added chemicals or coatings.[4]

items.title: Supercomputer Aids Design of Cheaper, Longer-Lasting Sodium-Enhanced Batteries
items.summary: Using the Expanse supercomputer, researchers advanced sodium-enhanced battery designs for cheaper, longer-lasting energy storage, potentially benefiting EVs and consumer electronics.[4]

items.title: Tiny Ultrasonic Tags Enable Battery-Free Smart Home Tracking
items.summary: Georgia Tech researchers developed penny-sized, battery-free ultrasonic tags that turn everyday objects into private smart home trackers using sound waves.[4]
